help: Legal given name as it appears on official documents (passport, institutional records).
Legal family name as it appears on official documents.
Name you prefer to be called, if different from your legal name. Leave blank if not applicable.
Use the email address assigned by your research institution or organization. Do not use a personal email address.
Country where you currently reside.
State or province where you currently reside.
City where you currently reside.

URL that helps verify your affiliation—for example an institutional profile, staff directory entry, or research group page. If you have no public profile, briefly explain how affiliation can be verified.
Optional. Your ORCID iD (Open Researcher and Contributor ID), e.g. 0000-0002-1825-0097. See https://orcid.org.
Upload your CV or résumé (usually PDF or Word). Some forms also use this field for area of expertise or publication history.

options: Business communication; Civic communication; Climate and environment; Computer science; Coordinated inauthentic behavior; Digital culture; Education; Election monitoring; Fact-checking; Hate speech and extremism; Health communication; Journalism, news and media; Marketing; Other; Political communication; Public health; Science communication; Trust and safety
help: Select the discipline or area of expertise that best describes your research background.
Full legal name of your employing or affiliated organization. If not listed in a dropdown, enter the name manually.
Department, school, lab, or research unit. Enter "N/A" if your institution has no separate department.

options: Academic institution; Government agency; Independent research organization (IRO); Intergovernmental organization (IGO); Non-governmental organization (NGO); Not-for-profit body, organization or association (based in the EU 27 Member States only); Other
help: Organisation category (e.g. university, NGO, government agency, EU not-for-profit research body). Used across Meta, TikTok, X, and similar forms.
Street address of the organization.
City where the organization is located.
Postal or ZIP code of the organization.
State, province, or region of the organization.
Country where the organization is legally based.
Public homepage of the organization (full URL including https://).
Your role or job title within the organization (e.g. PhD student, postdoctoral researcher, faculty).
Upload documentation that supports your affiliation (e.g. endorsement letter, appointment letter). Check platform rules for accepted file types and size limits.
Describe your organization's affiliation (e.g. university, lab, NGO) and not-for-profit or public-interest status, where relevant.

Confirm whether the research is conducted independently of commercial interests. Be prepared to substantiate with evidence (mission, governance, funding, prior research, organizational ties) if the platform requests it.

question_en: Did you receive funding for this research?
help: Answer Yes if any grant sponsor or institutional funding supports this research project.
Disclose all funding for the research, including amounts and direct and indirect sources (grants, sponsors, institutional support).

Short, descriptive title for the research program or project (e.g. "Social media usage among European political parties"). Some platforms limit length (often around 60 characters) and may not allow changes after submission.
Keywords, tags, or subject categories that describe the research field or topic.

Describe how the project advances scientific knowledge or serves the public interest: main objectives, focus areas, and questions you aim to answer. Many platforms require substantial detail (often 2,000+ characters).
State the main research questions or hypotheses your project will address.
Describe your research design, methods, data sources, and analysis plan.
Explain how the research relates to detection, identification, or understanding of systemic risks in the Union under DSA Article 34(1). Reference specific risk categories where applicable.
Expected outputs and deliverables (e.g. publications, datasets, policy briefs, software).
Literature review summary, bibliography, or reference list supporting the proposal. Optional unless required.
Planned start date of the research project. Use the date format required by the platform (often MM/DD/YYYY).
Outline the project timeline, including major phases and milestones.

question_en: How do you plan to publish your research?
help: How you plan to publish or disseminate results (e.g. peer-reviewed articles, preprints, conferences, reports). Note any expected publication date if the platform ties data access to publication.
Upload ethics or institutional review board (IRB) approval or exemption, if required by your institution or the platform.

Specify which platform data you need (data types, fields, APIs, or products) and how they are essential to the research described above—including why the data are not reasonably available by other means.

question_en: Does your research require you to have access to data for users who are under the age of 18?
help: Indicate whether the research requires access to data relating to users under 18. Additional safeguards or restricted environments may apply.
Date from which you need access to platform data. Must be consistent with your project timeline.
Date until which you need access. Justify the duration; some platforms cap maximum access (e.g. two years).
Geographic scope of the data requested (countries, regions, or markets).
Explain why the requested access period and scope are necessary and proportionate to the research aims.
How often you will refresh or update the dataset during the project (e.g. daily, weekly, never).

question_en: Have you applied for access with the platform before?
help: Whether you have previously applied for or participated in this platform's researcher or API access program.
When you begin retaining platform data, or the start of the retention period. Some forms express this as a duration bucket instead of a date.
When retained platform data will be deleted or anonymized, or the end of the retention period.

Describe technical and organizational measures to protect personal data: access controls, encryption, storage location, purpose limitation, incident response, and who is responsible for GDPR compliance. Mention relevant certifications if applicable.
